hero.title: Tutorials
hero.description: Master every AdonERP workflow through short, practical, and business-focused tutorials designed for owners, managers, operators, accountants, and developers.

categoriesSection.description: Pick a topic, follow the guided lesson, and apply the workflow to your real business process immediately.
categoriesSection.searchPlaceholder: Search tutorials by app, workflow, role, or difficulty...

categories.title: Getting Started
categories.description: Set up your workspace, users, company profile, apps, and basic access rules.

categories.title: Sales & CRM
categories.description: Manage leads, customers, quotations, pipelines, follow-ups, and sales reports.

categories.title: Inventory & Purchase
categories.description: Track products, stock moves, vendors, purchase orders, and replenishment rules.

categories.title: Accounting & Invoicing
categories.description: Create invoices, track payments, review taxes, and monitor financial activity.

categories.title: Project & Tasks
categories.description: Organize tasks, teams, approvals, deadlines, and performance dashboards.

categories.title: Developer Guides
categories.description: Learn API usage, customization patterns, modules, automation, and integrations.

featuredSection.description: These tutorials are selected for new teams who want to launch faster and understand the core AdonERP workflow from day one.

learningPathSection.description: Follow a clear path from setup to daily operation. Each lesson is built around real business actions, not theory only.
learningPathSection.stepDescription: Complete this step and move to the next recommended workflow.

ctaSection.description: Train your team with tutorials that connect learning, practice, and real operational workflows inside one business platform.

faqs.a: Yes. Each tutorial starts from the basics and then moves into practical business use cases.
Most business tutorials require no coding. Developer tutorials are clearly marked separately.

faqs.q: Can my team follow the same learning path?
faqs.a: Yes. Teams can use the tutorials to train sales, inventory, finance, support, and operations users.
Tutorials explain the practical flow, while documentation can be used for deeper reference.
